_comment: Cross-implementation parity test cases for the Traffic Cypher PM HTTP API.
Each case replays the same request sequence against the C and Rust
password manager binaries and compares normalized responses.
Schema (frozen — extend by adding cases, do not change keys):
name              string            human label, used in test output
requests          array<request>    HTTP requests replayed in order
normalize_drop    array<string>     JSON keys whose values are dropped
before comparison (timestamps,
ids, nonces, etc.)
expected_diff     bool | object     if true, mismatch is reported as
(optional)        KNOWN-DIVERGENT instead of FAIL.
May also be an object keyed by
BUILD_VARIANT (e.g. {"default":true,
"traffic_entropy":false}); the
runner resolves it to a scalar bool
using the active variant, falling
back to the "default" key or false.
reason            string (optional) why expected_diff is set
Request shape:
method            string            GET | POST | PUT | DELETE
path              string            URL path, may contain ${var} refs
body              any   (optional)  JSON request body
expect_status     int   (optional)  override the default 2xx check
save_token_from   string (optional) JSON key on the response to save
as ${token} for later substitution
(also adds Authorization: Bearer)
save_id_from      string (optional) JSON key on the response to save
as ${id} for later substitution

cases.name: streams_status
cases.reason: Default C build has the static 'Disabled' stub. With ENABLE_TRAFFIC_ENTROPY=1 (BUILD_VARIANT=traffic_entropy) the C build reads from multi_stream_manager_t and matches Rust ([] on a fresh PM). Tracked as #1a in REMEDIATION_PLAN.md.

cases.name: build_info
cases.reason: Default C build returns traffic_entropy:false; Rust returns true. With ENABLE_TRAFFIC_ENTROPY=1 (BUILD_VARIANT=traffic_entropy) the C build flips to traffic_entropy:true and matches. Note: build field itself ('c' vs 'rust') is dropped via normalize_drop.
